title: Dokkōdō — The Path of Walking Alone
sourceLang: Classical Japanese (Kumamoto Prefectural Museum of Art autograph, 1645; ja.wikisource kana-normalized text). Colophon: Shōhō 2, 5th month, 12th day — Shinmen Musashi Genshin (kaō), to Terao Magonojō-dono.

lines.src: 一 世々の道をそむく事なし
lines.amr: La ʿadiwa hinā na·pa·zamanāti.
lines.display: La ʿadiwa hinā na·pa·zamanāt·i.
lines.en: Oppose not the road of the ages.
lines.note: 世々 yoyo, age upon age — zaman (R-039) pluralized; そむく 'turn against' → ʿadiwa, the enmity-root's verb; Musashi opens with acceptance, and the Dokkodo opens where it will close: on a road

lines.src: 一 身にたのしみをたくまず
lines.amr: La ṭaliba niʿemya ra na·jisimi.
lines.display: La ṭaliba niʿem·ya ra na·jisim·i.
lines.en: Seek no pleasure for the body.
lines.note: niʿemi — the pleasure-word (N-ʿ-M, Ar. naʿima/naʿīm); たくむ 'scheme after' → ṭaliba, the ṭālib's own verb turned renunciant; 身 here is the flesh — jisim

lines.src: 一 よろずに依怙の心なし
lines.amr: En na·pa·šeyāti, la wi ib wikeli·i.
lines.display: En na·pa·šeyāt·i, la wi ib wikeli·i.
lines.en: In the myriad things, there is no heart of leaning.
lines.note: よろず myriad = na·pa·šeyāt — the Tao's own 萬物 (R-068); 依怙 eko, partial reliance → wikeli (W-K-L, the tawakkul-root): reliance on the partial is renounced; the trust has one Trustee (~Al-Wakīl, pairing #15)

lines.src: 一 身をあさく思ひ世をふかく思ふ
lines.amr: Ḥasiba na·nefesa naḏīs, ma ḥasiba na·dunyā ʿamīq.
lines.display: Ḥasiba na·nefes·a naḏīs, ma ḥasiba na·dunyā ʿamīq.
lines.en: Reckon the self shallow, and reckon the world deep.
lines.note: ḥasiba — the compute-root weighs values; naḏīs (N-Ḏ-S, Eg. nḏs 'small, lowly, commoner' — Wb 2:384, Faulkner 145) one sound from nefes: the lowly and the lower soul are kin; ʿamīq deep (ʿ-M-Q); dunyā (D-N-Y, ad-dunyā) — predicate adjectives caseless (R-026)

lines.src: 一 一生の間よくしん思はず
lines.amr: En na·ʿineki jamīʿi, la fakira šiweqya.
lines.display: En na·ʿineki jamīʿ·i, la fakira šiweq·ya.
lines.en: In the whole of life, think no craving-thought.
lines.note: ʿineki life and jamīʿ all — both free from the engine (ʿ-N-K abstract; J-M-ʿ adjective, exactly Arabic jamīʿ), zero coins; šiweqi — the same longing that served the Tao's 欲 (R-068): one desire-word across both Canon texts

lines.src: 一 我事におゐて後悔をせず
lines.amr: Bawt kiyiātkī, la ʾasifa.
lines.display: Bawt kiyiāt·kī, la ʾasifa.
lines.en: Over your own deeds, have no regret.
lines.note: 我事 my own affairs → kiyi deed (K-Y work-root abstract), plural by R-060's i+āt law; ʾ-S-F (R-049) was waiting for this line — Mi·ʾisef lives in the corpus, and the precept releases it

lines.src: 一 善悪に他をねたむ心なし
lines.amr: En harāy aw kapīli, la wi ib et·ḥasida na·ʾeḫarwa.
lines.display: En harāy aw kapīl·i, la wi ib et·ḥasida na·ʾeḫar·wa.
lines.en: In good or in ill, no heart that envies the other.
lines.note: ḥasida envy (Ḥ-S-D — Q 113:5's ḥāsid, the envier the dawn-surah takes refuge from); ʾeḫaru the other (ʾ-Ḫ-R) — and ʾaḫīr 'the last': Al-Āḫir joins Az-Zāhir and Al-Bāṭin, three of Q 57:3's four Names now spoken natively across two Canon texts (pairing #16)

lines.src: 一 いづれの道にもわかれをかなしまず
lines.amr: En na·pa·hināt jamīʿi, la ḥazina fireqya.
lines.display: En na·pa·hināt jamīʿ·i, la ḥazina fireq·ya.
lines.en: On all the roads, grieve not the parting.
lines.note: fireqi parting (F-R-Q — Ar. firāq, Q 75:28 'it is the parting'); every road includes the Way's own: Musashi grieves no farewell on any of them — precept 21 will pick this root back up

lines.src: 一 自他共にうらみかこつ心なし
lines.amr: Ra nefes ma ʾeḫarwi en·wa, la wi ib ḥiqedi·i la šikewi·i.
lines.display: Ra nefes ma ʾeḫar·wi en·wa, la wi ib ḥiqedi·i la šikewi·i.
lines.en: Toward self and other as one, no heart of grudge nor complaint.
lines.note: 共に carried by en·wa, the together-adverb (R-069) in its second Canon text; ḥiqedi grudge (Ḥ-Q-D, Ar. ḥiqd) and šikewi complaint (Š-K-W — Yaʿqūb's ʾaškū, Q 12:86: the Way complains only to Allah); la … la — the nor-coordination (R-014)

lines.src: 一 れんぼの道思ひよるこゝろなし
lines.amr: La wi ib et·mašiya ben hina miri·i.
lines.display: La wi ib et·mašiya ben hina miri·i.
lines.en: No heart that walks toward the road of love-longing.
lines.note: れんぼ renbo (恋慕, love-attachment) → miri (M-R): mrj unified love and desire in Egyptian as in Amraqiyyah — the root carries the middle precepts' renunciations from here through 13; 思ひよる 'incline toward' → the walking heart, et·mašiya ben

lines.src: 一 物毎にすきこのむ事なし
lines.amr: En šey ma šeyi, la wi miri la kirehi.
lines.display: En šey ma šey·i, la wi miri la kirehi.
lines.en: In thing after thing, no liking nor loathing.
lines.note: 物毎 'each single thing' — thing-and-thing, the distributive by coordination; the preference-pair rendered as love-nor-hate: to prefer nothing is to hold neither

lines.src: 一 私宅におゐてのぞむ心なし
lines.amr: Bawt hawti, la wi ib et·mara.
lines.display: Bawt hawt·i, la wi ib et·mara.
lines.en: Concerning a dwelling, no heart that desires.
lines.note: 私宅 the private house — hawt (T-1); Musashi gave away his possessions at Reigandō in the week these lines were written; mara — the want-verb of the same mrj root

lines.src: 一 身ひとつに美食をこのまず
lines.amr: Ra jisim waḥīdi, la mara wonimon jamīla.
lines.display: Ra jisim waḥīd·i, la mara wonimon jamīl·a.
lines.en: For the single body, crave no fine food.
lines.note: 身ひとつ 'the body being but one' → jisim waḥīd — the alone-word the title walks on (W-Ḥ-D, pairing #14 ~Al-Wāḥid); 美食 calqued exactly: wonimon food (W-N-M patient, already in the corpus) + jamīl beautiful (J-M-L)

lines.src: 一 末々代物なる古き道具を所持せず
lines.amr: La wi ʿindki sakimet qadīm ṯamīn.
lines.display: La wi ʿind·ki sakimet qadīm ṯamīn.
lines.en: Keep no old and treasured implement.
lines.note: ʿind-possession negated existentially (R-014 × R-027): 'let there not be, at you, …'; sakimet — the instrument-noun of S-K-M power: a tool is a power-thing, and Musashi keeps none but one; qadīm old (Q-D-M), ṯamīn precious (R-053)

lines.src: 一 わが身にいたり物いみする事なし
lines.amr: Ra nefeskī, la ʿabida ṭiyerya.
lines.display: Ra nefes·kī, la ʿabida ṭiyer·ya.
lines.en: For your own self, serve no omen.
lines.note: 物忌み monoimi — taboo-observance, omen-avoidance → ṭiyeri (Ṭ-Y-R, Ar. ṭiyara: the ḥadīṯ's lā ṭiyara — Musashi's anti-superstition precept and the Sunnah use one word); ʿabida 'serve/worship' makes the renunciation theological: a superstition served is a god taken

lines.src: 一 兵具は各別よの道具たしなまず
lines.amr: Na·ḫepeš waḥīd — la mara sakimet ʾaḫīra.
lines.display: Na·ḫepeš waḥīd — la mara sakimet ʾaḫīr·a.
lines.en: The sword stands alone — crave no other implement.
lines.note: ḫepeš (Ḫ-P-Š — Eg. ḫpš: the khopesh-blade, the strong arm, strength itself; Wb 3:268, Allen): the sword-saint's 兵具 lands on Kemet's own sword; 各別 'set apart' → waḥīd again — the blade shares the walker's aloneness

lines.src: 一 道におゐてハ死をいとはず思ふ
lines.amr: En al·hinay, fakira na·mawta bilā kirehī.
lines.display: En al·hinay, fakira na·mawt·a bilā kirehī.
lines.en: On THE Way, think on death without loathing.
lines.note: mawt (M-W-T) — the mortality field opens at last; with nefes and ḏawiqa standing, Q 3:185 can now be spoken: every nafs shall taste death; al·hina wears the sacred article as the Tao's Way does (R-069) — for Musashi, 道 here is not one road among many; いとはず → bilā kirehī, without loathing

lines.src: 一 老身に財宝所領もちゆる心なし
lines.amr: Ra na·simesī, la wi ib et·jamiʿa fulusa ma yakāta.
lines.display: Ra na·simesī, la wi ib et·jamiʿa fulus·a ma yakāt·a.
lines.en: For old age, no heart that gathers money and lands.
lines.note: simesi — old age, the abstract the elder-root S-M-S held in reserve (derived, not coined); 財宝所領 wealth-and-fiefs → fulus (T-8) and yakāt (R-056); jamiʿa — the gather-verb whose adjective jamīʿ walks through this text as 'all': gathering renounced, wholeness kept

lines.src: 一 佛神は貴し佛神をたのまず
lines.amr: Na·pa·ʾilahāt karīm — we·pen la wakila ʿala na·pa·ʾilahāti.
lines.display: Na·pa·ʾilahāt karīm — we·pen la wakila ʿala na·pa·ʾilahāt·i.
lines.en: The gods are noble — yet lean not upon the gods.
lines.note: 佛神 the buddhas-and-kami → na·pa·ʾilahāt, the generic deity-word that exists to be negated in the shahada (R-040); wakila (W-K-L, precept 3's root; tawakkala ʿalā takes ʿala in Arabic as here): Musashi's line is shahada-shaped — honor what is noble, entrust nothing to it; in the Way's mouth the precept completes itself: reliance belongs to Al-Wakīl alone. ALTERNATIVE for Ali: na·pa·qadīsāt 'the holy ones' if ʾilahāt reads too strong

lines.src: 一 身を捨ても名利はすてず
lines.amr: Hajira na·jisima — we·pen la hajira na·rinya.
lines.display: Hajira na·jisim·a — we·pen la hajira na·rin·ya.
lines.en: Forsake the body — yet forsake not the name.
lines.note: hajira forsake (H-J-R — the hijra-root: departure as devotion, Q 73:10's 'beautiful forsaking'); 名利 meiri — fame-and-gain in the letter, honor in the received reading → rini, the REN (R-040): 'to speak a name is to keep a soul alive' — Musashi's most Egyptian line: the body dies, the ren endures. kiremi (honor) stands as alternative

lines.src: 一 常に兵法の道をはなれず
lines.amr: La·wayen la fariqa min al·hina ḫepeši.
lines.display: La·wayen la fariqa min al·hina ḫepeš·i.
lines.en: Never part from THE road of the sword.
lines.note: 兵法の道 the way of heihō → al·hina ḫepeš·i, the Road of the Sword (Ḫ-P-Š closing what precept 16 opened); la·wayen + la — negative concord sealing the vow (R-014); 常 was dahīr in the Tao's first line, and an alternate reading stands: En dahr, la fariqa… 'in the aeon, part not'; the last precept answers the first — the road not opposed is the road never left
